params.description: 2 stacks of 5 items appear. Running total shows: Total: 5, Total: 10.

steps.text: 2 stacks of 5 makes 10, not 15. Skip count by 5s until you reach 15. How many times did you count?

params.description: 4 stacks of 5 items appear. Running total shows: Total: 5, Total: 10, Total: 15, Total: 20.

steps.text: 4 stacks of 5 makes 20, too many. We need 15. How many 5s does it take to reach exactly 15?

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 (5 × 5 = 25, not 15; may have confused items per stack with number of stacks)

params.description: 5 stacks of 5 items appear. Running total shows: Total: 5, Total: 10, Total: 15, Total: 20, Total: 25.

steps.text: 5 stacks of 5 makes 25, not 15. The unknown is how many stacks you need. Skip count by 5s and stop at 15. How many times did you count?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates skip counting. First stack appears: 5. Second stack appears: 10. Third stack appears: 15. Final groups show 3 stacks of 5.

steps.text: Let me show you. Skip count by 5s. First stack: 5. Second stack: 10. Third stack: 15. I counted three times. 3 stacks of 5 equals 15. That's the unknown.
